mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in initial unemployment claims (UNRATE) above 1.0% in a month signals deteriorating labor market conditions, prompting risk-off behavior that drives S&P 500 forward returns lower over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 5d return volatility in triggered periods falls below 15%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate weakening economic demand, creating downside pressure on equity valuations through reduced corporate earnings expectations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where both GDPC1 and HOUST decline, or the joint signal's Rank IC < 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 3-month moving average signals improving supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive SPX returns through enhanced corporate profit margins over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances where INDPRO exceeds its 3-month MA, or the signal's Sharpe ratio falls below 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: A negative real interest rate (FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL) below 1.5% indicates accommodative financial conditions that boost equity valuations via increased risk appetite, producing positive SPX returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL < 1.5%, or the interaction's Rank IC < 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and rise in unemployment (UNRATE) signals a labor market slowdown, creating negative SPX return pressure through heightened recession risk over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises, or the joint signal's t-statistic < 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in consumer spending (RSAFS) above its 6-month trend indicates strong demand-side momentum, generating positive SPX returns through elevated corporate revenue expectations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of instances where RSAFS exceeds its 6-month trend, or the signal's IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month low while capacity utilization (INDPRO) remains above 80% signals overheating supply chains, creating negative SPX return pressure through inflationary risks over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances meeting the trigger condition, or the signal's Sharpe ratio < 0.2.
